title: Problem 1.1
statement_text: Determine the smallest $m$ such that in every $2$-coloring of the edges of the ordered complete graph $K_{m}$, there is a monochromatic non-nested matching of size $n$.
context_text: Two independent edges $ij$ and $st$ in an ordered complete graph are nested if $i<s<t<j$ or $s<i<j<t$. The answer is known to lie between $3n-1$ and $4n-2$; the presenting author conjectured (in prior work [2]) that the lower bound $3n-1$ is optimal. A Turán-type approach recently improved the upper bound to $(2+\sqrt{3})n$.
paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings

title: Problem 2.1
statement_text: Let $G$ be a random 4-regular graph. Is it true that the coefficient of $P_{G}$ at $\prod_{v\in V(G)}v^{2}$ is a.a.s. non-zero?
context_text: The graph polynomial $P_G$ is uniform and every monomial appearing with a non-zero coefficient has total degree $|E(G)|$; for a 4-regular graph the only relevant monomial is $\prod_{v\in V(G)}v^{2}$. The expected value of the coefficient is zero in the two Hamiltonian cycles model, so a proof would require an anti-concentration inequality.
paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings

title: Problem 3.1
statement_text: Let $G$ be a disjoint union of cycles. Let $H$ be a disjoint union of $K_{4}$'s. Is $G\cup H$ 4-colourable?
context_text: This is a special case of the general strong chromatic number question: if $G$ has maximum degree $d$ and $H$ is a disjoint union of $K_{2d}$'s, is $G\cup H$ $(2d)$-colourable? The quantity $2d$ would be best possible, and it is known that $G\cup H$ is $(3d-1)$-colourable when $H$ is a disjoint union of $K_{3d-1}$'s.
paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings

title: Problem 4.1
statement_text:
Is it true that every diamond-free claw-free cubic graph is strongly 6-edge-colorable?
In other words, is it true that $\chi^{\prime}_{s}(T(G))=6$ for every cubic graph $G$?
context_text: Lv, Li, and Zhang proved that every claw-free subcubic graph other than the triangular prism satisfies $\chi^{\prime}_{s}(G)\leq 8$. A partial solution by Han and Cui establishes strong 6-edge-colorability for truncated prisms.
paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings

title: Problem 5.1
statement_text:
If a cubic graph admits a $2$-homogeneous coloring,
then $4$ colors are always sufficient.
context_text: A $k$-homogeneous coloring is a proper vertex coloring in which exactly $k$ colors appear in the open neighborhood of every vertex. For bipartite cubic graphs it is known that at most 6 colors suffice for a 2-homogeneous coloring; the conjecture asks whether 4 always suffice whenever a 2-homogeneous coloring exists.
notes: Stated as a declarative conjecture inside a labeled Problem environment; a weaker bipartite version appeared in prior work [2].
paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings

title: Problem 5.2
statement_text: There is a finite number of connected bridgeless cubic graphs that do not admit a $2$-homogeneous coloring.
context_text: Not every cubic graph admits a 2-homogeneous coloring; for example, a graph containing a triangular prism with one edge of a triangle subdivided does not. The conjecture claims the set of exceptions is finite.
notes: Stated as a declarative conjecture inside a labeled Problem environment.
paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings

title: Conjecture 6.1
statement_text: Each bridgeless graph admits a $2$-flow $\varphi_{2}$ and a $4$-flow $\varphi_{4}$ such that for any edge $e$, if $\varphi_{2}(e)=0$, then $|\varphi_{4}(e)|\geq 2$.
context_text: The conjecture implies the 5-flow conjecture since $(5\varphi_{2}+\varphi_{4})/2$ is a circular 5-flow, and generalises Seymour's approach in the proof of the 6-flow theorem. The resulting pair is called a $1/2$-flow-pair, and the conjecture has been verified computationally for all cyclically 4-edge-connected snarks up to 34 vertices.
paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings

title: Problem 7.1
statement_text: Every bicritical snark is almost-$\mathbb{Z}_{2}^{2}$-connected and almost-$\mathbb{Z}_{4}$-connected.
context_text: A graph is almost-$A$-connected if all boundaries are admissible for group $A$ except $\partial f=0$. The property has been verified for the Petersen graph and both Blanuša snarks on 18 vertices. The conjecture emerged from exploration of the House of Graphs snark database.
notes: Stated as a declarative conjecture inside a labeled Problem environment.
paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings
